howTo.name: How to use the mm to px converter
howTo.description: Instructions: mm to pixels at DPI.

contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert mm to pixels?
contentBlocks.html:
In precision design work, dimensions are often specified in millimeters. To prepare digital images, you need to convert mm to pixels at the target DPI: pixels = mm × DPI / 25.4.

This conversion is essential for packaging design, label printing, product photography, and any work requiring precise mm-to-pixel mapping.

At 300 DPI: 1 mm = 11.81 pixels, 85 mm (business card width) = 1,004 pixels.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.title: 1. Enter a value
items.description: Type a number in the input field. You can use a period or comma as the decimal separator.

items.title: 2. Read the result
items.description: The conversion result appears instantly in the field next to it – no clicking required.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse
items.description: Click Copy result or use the Reverse button to convert in the opposite direction.

contentBlocks.title: How does mm to pixel conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
The formula is: pixels = mm × DPI / 25.4. Since 1 inch = 25.4 mm exactly.

At 300 DPI: 1 mm = 11.811 pixels. At 600 DPI: 1 mm = 23.622 pixels.

For bleed area: add 3 mm (standard) to each edge. A 100 × 50 mm design with 3 mm bleed = 106 × 56 mm = 1,252 × 661 px at 300 DPI.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Business card: 85 × 55 mm + 3 mm bleed = 91 × 61 mm = 1,075 × 720 px at 300 DPI.
  • Standard label: 50 × 25 mm at 300 DPI = 591 × 295 px.
  • Wine label: 90 × 120 mm at 300 DPI = 1,063 × 1,417 px.
  • Minimum text: 8pt font needs at least 150 DPI to be readable.

contentBlocks.title: MM to pixels at various DPI

items.question: How many pixels is 1 mm at 300 DPI?
items.answer: 1 mm at 300 DPI = 11.811 pixels. Formula: mm × 300 / 25.4.

items.question: What is bleed and how do I calculate it?
items.answer: Bleed is extra area (typically 3 mm) beyond the trim edge to prevent white borders. Add 3 mm to each edge and convert to pixels.

items.question: What DPI for business cards?
items.answer: 300 DPI minimum. Some printers request 600 DPI for small text and fine details.

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
